title: Infosys unveils AI First value framework targeting USD 300-400 billion AI services opportunity by 2030
detailed_summary: Infosys unveiled an AI First value framework intended to help global enterprises unlock AI value at scale using its generative and agentic AI suite, Infosys Topaz™. The company said this opens a new frontier in IT services and positions Infosys to tap an incremental AI first services opportunity of USD 300-400 billion by 2030, citing a recent Nasscom - McKinsey Report. Infosys described a two-pronged AI strategy: capturing new demand across six value pools and augmenting existing services with AI to expand wallet share. It said it is collaborating with 90 percent of its top 200 clients on AI journeys, has more than 4600 AI projects underway, and has developed over 30 new service offerings across the six value pools.

key_facts.fact: Infosys unveiled its AI first value framework to help global enterprises unlock AI value at scale using Infosys Topaz™.
Infosys said the framework will enable the company to tap into an incremental AI first services opportunity of USD 300-400 billion by 2030, according to a recent Nasscom - McKinsey Report.
Infosys described a two-pronged AI strategy: capture new demand for AI first services across six value pools and augment existing services with AI to expand wallet share.
The six AI value pools identified are AI Strategy & Engineering, Data for AI, Process AI, Agentic Legacy Modernization, Physical AI, and AI Trust.
Infosys said it is collaborating with 90 percent of its top 200 clients on their AI journeys.
Infosys said it has more than 4600 AI projects underway.
Infosys said it has developed over 30 new service offerings across the six value pools.

financial_metrics.guidance: incremental AI first services opportunity of USD 300-400 billion by 2030

partnerships_or_deals.description: Infosys said it is collaborating with 90 percent of its top 200 clients on their AI journeys.
Infosys referred to a strategic AI-first collaboration with GE Vernova.
